quick_facts.label: Professional classification
quick_facts.value: Operation Theatre (OT) Technologists are included within the Surgical and Anaesthesia-related Technology professional category in the National Commission for Allied and Healthcare Professions Act, 2021.

quick_facts.label: Senior-role scope
quick_facts.value: A Government of India hospital duty roster defines a Senior Operation Theatre Technician as performing OT Technician work while assisting the OT Supervisor administratively, maintaining instruments and emergency outfits, supervising subordinate staff, and compiling monthly operation statistics.

quick_facts.label: Published occupational benchmark
quick_facts.value: The Healthcare Sector Skill Council’s published Operating Theatre Technician qualification pack, HSS/Q2601, places the base OTT role at NSQF level 4 and lists Class XII science as the minimum educational qualification; the document describes this as a base-role standard rather than a universal requirement for every employer’s senior designation.

quick_facts.label: Example public-sector progression rule
quick_facts.value: For Kalawati Saran Children’s Hospital, New Delhi, the 2026 recruitment rules provide for Senior Operation Theatre Technician by promotion from an Operation Theatre Technician in pay level 4 after five years of regular service. This is an employer-specific rule, not a nationwide promotion guarantee.

quick_facts.label: Government pay context
quick_facts.value: In the Kalawati Saran Children’s Hospital recruitment rules published on April 4, 2026, the single Senior Operation Theatre Technician post was Group C, non-gazetted and non-ministerial at pay-matrix Level 5 (₹29,200–₹92,300). Pay, allowances, vacancies and eligibility vary by employer and recruitment notice.

sections.heading: Responsibilities
paragraphs: A Senior Operation Theatre Technician combines hands-on theatre work with coordination and oversight. In a Government of India hospital duty roster, the role includes all underlying OT Technician duties, administrative support to the Operation Theatre Supervisor, custody and upkeep of instruments and appliances for the main, recovery and emergency theatres, ensuring emergency outfits remain functional, supervision of subordinate staff, and monthly reporting of major and minor procedures.

The underlying OTT work includes keeping anaesthetic and surgical equipment functional, supporting sterile-supply and medical-gas systems, reporting equipment downtime, positioning and transferring patients safely, assisting with anaesthesia as assigned, maintaining theatre cleanliness and sterility, following universal precautions, and complying with biomedical-waste requirements. Exact task allocation remains subject to hospital policy, clinical supervision and the technician’s authorised scope of practice.
bullets: Coordinate theatre readiness: instruments, equipment, consumables, emergency arrangements and safe operating conditions.
Guide and supervise junior technical staff; escalate operational issues to the OT Supervisor or medical officer.
Maintain administrative records such as procedure statistics, equipment information and stock-related documentation.
Support safe surgical workflows without substituting for the surgeon, anaesthetist or other licensed clinician.

sections.heading: Entry routes
paragraphs: The usual route is to first qualify and work as an Operation Theatre Technician or OT Technologist, then gain the experience and supervisory capability required by a particular hospital. HSSC’s published OTT qualification pack identifies Class XII science and a relevant professional qualification as the base-role educational benchmark, while its listed occupational standards cover theatre preparation, equipment support, sterile-field practice, infection control, patient preparation, intra-operative technical support, emergencies, supplies, quality and safe working.

Academic routes are not uniform across India. For example, universities advertise B.Sc. programmes in Operation Theatre Technology or Anaesthesia and OT Technology for students completing 10+2 science, commonly with Physics, Chemistry and Biology or Mathematics; programme duration, internship structure, entrance tests and marks thresholds are set by the individual university and may change by admission cycle.

For a senior government post, promotion rules may be decisive. At Kalawati Saran Children’s Hospital, the 2026 rule specifies promotion from an OT Technician in Level 4 after five years of regular service, with no direct-recruit qualification prescribed for that post. Candidates should therefore check the recruitment rules, service rules and vacancy notice of the target employer rather than assume a single India-wide pathway.
bullets: Complete an employer-accepted OTT/OT-technology qualification and clinical training.
Build documented experience as an OT Technician, including safe equipment, sterile-workflow and patient-preparation practice.
Develop evidence of dependable shift coordination, stock/equipment control, records and junior-staff supervision.
Read the specific hospital’s recruitment rules before applying, especially whether the senior post is promotional, direct recruitment or both.

sections.heading: Skills and competencies
paragraphs: Core technical competence includes selecting, setting up, connecting, testing, cleaning, storing and maintaining theatre equipment; recognising minor faults and referring equipment for specialist maintenance when needed. HSSC also identifies knowledge of operating-room terminology, relevant anatomy and physiology, surgical procedures, patient safety, asepsis, infection control, biomedical-waste disposal, work health and safety, equipment documentation and manufacturer instructions.

At senior level, the role also calls for operational leadership: prioritising readiness across routine and emergency work, communicating clearly with surgeons, anaesthesia teams, nurses and technicians, supervising staff, maintaining records and handling administrative work. These capabilities matter because the senior technician’s duties sit between direct technical work and the OT Supervisor’s coordination role.
bullets: Aseptic technique, infection-prevention practice and biomedical-waste compliance.
Anaesthetic and surgical equipment readiness, basic fault identification, maintenance logs and escalation.
Patient-safe transfer and positioning, equipment safety and emergency-outfit readiness.
Clear communication, documentation, delegation, staff supervision and teamwork under time pressure.

sections.heading: Workplace and working pattern
paragraphs: The role is based in surgical-service environments: the main operation theatre, recovery room and emergency operation theatre. The associated work also interfaces with theatre sterile and supply arrangements, medical-gas systems, equipment maintenance and emergency preparedness.

Work is team-based and safety-critical. The HSSC occupational standard describes OTTs as preparing and maintaining theatres and equipment while assisting surgical and anaesthetic teams; it also specifies collaboration with doctors, nurses and other healthcare providers. Senior technicians additionally supervise technical staff and assist with theatre administration under the hospital’s hierarchy.
bullets: Typical employers include public hospitals, medical colleges, specialty hospitals and private hospitals with operating-theatre services.
Shift, emergency and on-call expectations depend on the hospital’s surgical workload and staffing model.
The senior designation and day-to-day authority are employer-specific; hospital policy determines delegation, reporting lines and permitted duties.

sections.heading: Progression
paragraphs: A practical progression pattern is OT Technician to Senior Operation Theatre Technician and then, where the employer has such a post, Operation Theatre Supervisor or a comparable theatre-management role. The DGHS duty roster distinguishes these levels: the Senior OT Technician supports the Supervisor administratively and supervises subordinate staff, while the Supervisor has broader responsibility for deployment of technical staff, equipment continuity, inventories, sterile supply, medical gases and safety systems.

Promotion is not standardised nationally. The Kalawati Saran Children’s Hospital rules notified in 2026 make the senior post promotional from OT Technician after five years of regular service in Level 4. Other employers may use different experience thresholds, qualifications, selection processes or post titles.
bullets: Strengthen capability in equipment coordination, inventory, documentation and safety systems.
Seek supervised responsibility for junior-staff guidance and theatre readiness across routine and emergency lists.
Check whether an employer’s next step is titled Senior OTT, OT Supervisor, Theatre Manager or another local designation.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Compensation is highly employer-, state-, city-, contract- and allowance-dependent. As a dated public-sector reference, the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare’s recruitment rules published on April 4, 2026 classified one Senior Operation Theatre Technician post at Kalawati Saran Children’s Hospital, New Delhi as Level 5 in the central pay matrix, ₹29,200–₹92,300; the post count was stated as one, subject to variation with workload.

This pay-matrix reference should not be used as a national salary estimate. Private hospitals, state-government services, outsourced roles and institutions using different service rules may offer materially different fixed pay, benefits, shift compensation and promotion-linked pay.
bullets: Use the applicable vacancy notification or service rules as the authoritative compensation source.
Confirm whether pay is on a central/state pay matrix, a hospital scale, a contract rate or an outsourced-service arrangement.
Assess total compensation alongside shifts, emergency duty, allowances, leave, benefits and progression rules.
